title: styphelia woodsii
intro: Styphelia woodsii is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Epacridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and stems, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Styphelia woodsii has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. It can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
